channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Almost 40 percent of Americans report facing extreme weather: Survey
item.description: Nearly 40 percent of Americans experienced extreme weather last year — and those who did were more likely to be concerned about climate change, according to new polling.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, KREX 5

item.title: Control of the Wisconsin Supreme Court is at stake in race that's drawn powerful political interests
item.description: MADISON, Wis. (AP) — Majority control of the Wisconsin Supreme Court will be decided Tuesday in a race that broke records for spending and has become a proxy battle for the nation’s political fights, pitting a candidate backed by President Donald Trump against a Democratic-aligned challenger.
item.dc:creator: SCOTT BAUER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Wisconsin voters to choose top education official and consider voter ID amendment
item.description: Wisconsin is electing its top education official, who will guide policies affecting K-12 schools during President Donald Trump’s second term.
item.dc:creator: Scott Bauer, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Battle over trans athletes' participation in girls sports comes to California Capitol
item.description: A committee in the California legislature plans to vote on bills Tuesday that would restrict transgender athletes from participating in girls sports.
item.dc:creator: Sophie Austin, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Evacuations ordered as winds pose challenges for firefighters battling California blaze
item.description: BISHOP, Calif. (AP) — Firefighters stopped the forward spread of a wildfire tearing through a remote area of eastern California, but evacuation orders for residents of hundreds of homes remained in place, officials said Monday evening.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: NASA's newly returned astronauts say they would fly on Boeing's Starliner capsule again
item.description: CAPE CANAVERAL, Fla. (AP) — NASA’s celebrity astronauts Butch Wilmore and Suni Williams said Monday that they hold themselves partly responsible for what went wrong on their space sprint-turned-marathon and would fly on Boeing's Starliner again.
item.dc:creator: MARCIA DUNN, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: A court is allowing the release of most records in the Gene Hackman death investigation
item.description: SANTA FE, N.M. (AP) — A court on Monday cleared the way for the release of investigative records from the deaths of Gene Hackman and his wife, Betsy Arakawa, as long as depictions of the deceased couple are blocked from view.
item.dc:creator: MORGAN LEE, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Colorado man federally charged in Tesla firebomb attack: 'Justice is coming,' AG Pam Bondi says
item.description: A Colorado man arrested in Texas for allegedly firebombing a Tesla dealership in Colorado is facing federal charges in addition to state charges.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Girl, 9, dies after procedure at California dental office
item.description: A report from the San Diego County Medical Examiner's Office claims the girl was discharged from the facility and remained asleep on the ride home.
item.dc:creator: Delaney White, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Nonprofit groups and Democrats sue Trump administration over election executive order
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — President Donald Trump's executive order seeking to overhaul the nation's elections faced its first legal challenges Monday as the Democratic National Committee and a pair of nonprofits filed two separate lawsuits calling it unconstitutional.
item.dc:creator: ALI SWENSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 20 staffers accused of abusing students or not reporting it at Pennsylvania charter school
item.description: Twenty staffers at a suburban Philadelphia charter school are facing charges related to the alleged physical abuse of students using painful, unapproved techniques to restrain and punish youths in a program meant to help them deal with emotional issues, authorities announced Monday.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SHIPKOWSKI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: A year later, Super Bowl's tourism impact on Las Vegas clear to see
item.description: Las Vegas tourism indicators were down across the board in February, but the reason was pretty easy to see in comparisons to February 2024.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: CBP in Texas confiscates 242 pounds of smuggled Mexican bologna at port of entry
item.description: A man was stopped for the second time in two months, allegedly trying to smuggle several pounds of Mexican bologna into the U.S. through an El Paso, Texas port.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump has dubbed April 2 'Liberation Day' for his tariffs. Here's what to expect
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — As the trade wars launched by U.S. President Donald Trump continue to escalate, all eyes are on Wednesday. Trump has repeatedly called April 2 “Liberation Day,” with promises to roll out a set of tariffs, or taxes on imports from other countries, that he says will free the U.S.
item.dc:creator: WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Georgia lawmakers agree on school safety bill after Apalachee High School shooting
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — Georgia lawmakers said repeatedly that a student-tracking database had been removed from a school safety bill, but parts of that plan survive.
item.dc:creator: JEFF AMY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: UPDATE: Dothan students injured after suspected tornado hits school
item.description: DOTHAN, Ala (WDHN) -- A suspected tornado hit the downtown area of Dothan, Alabama, Monday afternoon, damaging a portion of Dothan Preparatory Academy. Dothan City School officials confirmed to WDHN that 5 students sustained minor and moderate injuries when the severe weather hit the school.
item.dc:creator: Richard Everett, NewsNation

item.title: Under the new Trump administration, this year’s Transgender Day of Visibility has a different tenor
item.description: On the campaign trail, Donald Trump used contentiousness around transgender people's access to sports and bathrooms to fire up conservative voters and sway undecideds.
item.dc:creator: GEOFF MULVIHILL and JESSE BEDAYN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Increasing sleepiness linked to dementia risk in women, study finds
item.description: The research by Neurology discovered women in their eighties who experience sleep disruption have an association with cognitive aging.
item.dc:creator: Diana Falzone, NewsNation

item.title: Trump hosts Ramadan dinner at White House
item.description: “The Muslim community was there for us in November, and while I’m president, I’ll be there for you,” Trump said.
item.dc:creator: Susie Pinto, NewsNation

item.title: Texas man sues deputies who shot him while on the phone with dispatchers
item.description: The interaction caught on body camera footage is now the subject of a federal lawsuit alleging the two deputies used excessive force.
item.dc:creator: Kelly Wiley, NewsNation

item.title: Trump is stronger on immigration and weaker on trade, an AP-NORC poll finds
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Immigration remains a strength for President Donald Trump, but his handling of tariffs is getting more negative feedback, according to a poll from The Associated Press-NORC Center for Public Affairs Research. About half of U.S.
item.dc:creator: AMELIA THOMSON-DEVEAUX and LINLEY SANDERS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Epstein victim Virginia Giuffre says she has four days to live
item.description: Giuffre didn’t disclose when the accident happened, but said it has been the “worst start to the new year.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 3 US soldiers found dead in armored vehicle in Lithuania; 1 still missing
item.description: Three of the U.S. Army soldiers who went missing in Lithuania have been found dead in their armored vehicle that was pulled from a swampy area early Monday, according to U.S. Army Europe and Africa Command. Another soldier is still missing.
item.dc:creator: By LIUDAS DAPKUS and LOLITA C. BALDOR Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Driver facing multiple charges in Brooklyn crash that killed mother, two children
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A woman is facing multiple charges after she crashed into a family out for a walk in Brooklyn, killing a mother and her two children in what New York City Mayor Eric Adams described as a “tragic accident of a Shakespearean proportion.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Masked Tesla vandal wrecks Cybertruck, leaves note for homeowner in attack caught on video
item.description: California police are searching for two suspects after separate incidents involving Tesla vehicles being vandalized marked the latest acts of violence against the brand across the country.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Denny Hamlin ends 10-year win drought at Martinsville Speedway
item.description: MARTINSVILLE, Va. (AP) — At the track he loves to dominate, Denny Hamlin was back on top with a new face atop his pit box.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Min Woo Lee holds off Scheffler and Woodland to win Houston Open for first PGA Tour title
item.description: Min Woo Lee kept his calm amid tremendous charges by Scottie Scheffler and Gary Woodland, winning the Houston Open on Sunday for his first PGA Tour title with the best lag of his life that set up a final par for a one-shot victory.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Woman receives 100 cancer treatments, living far beyond doctors' expectations
item.description: An 84-year-old Utah woman recently received her 100th cancer treatment, outliving doctors' expectations.
item.dc:creator: Yeonseung Kim, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Figure skater who lost both parents in DC plane crash brings world championships crowd to its feet
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — Maxim Naumov came to a stop in the middle of the ice, looked up at the sky and patted his heart. Then he mouthed a few words, in Russian, to his parents: “This is for you guys. You guys are with me. I love you both.
item.dc:creator: JIMMY GOLEN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: See a missing police officer post? It's likely part of a nationwide scam, authorities say
item.description: Have you seen a post on Facebook recently, asking you to be on the lookout for a missing police officer? Chances are, you're actually seeing a scam post, authorities say.
item.dc:creator: Isaiah Calalang, WGN 9

item.title: Trump 'very angry' at Putin's remarks on Zelensky
item.description: The president threatened to slap a new tariff on Russia if it is at fault for stalling an end "to bloodshed.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, Fox 5

item.title: Delta Air Lines drops new set of pilot trading cards: How to get yours
item.description: Trading card collectors and aviation fanatics will soon have an exclusive set of cards to begin collecting on Delta Air Lines flights this year.
item.dc:creator: Derick Fox, Fox 5

item.title: Who is Trevor Milton, the startup founder and donor pardoned by Trump
item.description: Milton said Thursday in a post on social platform X that he spoke to Trump over the phone and that he signed his pardon of “innocence.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WGN 9
